description: An India-linked supertanker, Sarv Shakti, has successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy corridor under intense pressure due to Middle East tensions. This passage marks a significant breakthrough for India, the world's second-largest LPG consumer, as it secures essential cooking fuel amid disrupted supply chains and a US blockade on Iran-linked shipping.

How parking disputes are turning into deadly violence across cities
description: Parking disputes in Delhi and Bengaluru are escalating into violence, with recent incidents involving assaults, firing, and even deaths. These conflicts, often triggered by minor issues like a misplaced vehicle, highlight a growing shortage of space and increasingly confrontational attitudes among residents.

‘I am vegetarian so need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: Snap AI coding shift: Evan Spiegel says Claude now writes most new code
description: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel is impressed by AI's rapid advancement, noting tools like Anthropic's Claude are transforming software development. He predicts companies will reallocate resources from engineering to distribution as AI automates coding, with over two-thirds of new code at Snap now written by AI systems.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: Laura Loomer warns Candace Owens of lawsuit in Erika Kirk row
description: Candace Owens is facing a potential lawsuit threat from Laura Loomer after refusing to retract claims involving Erika Kirk and the 2025 killing of Charlie Kirk. Owens insists she stands by her statements, while Loomer warned legal action is imminent. The dispute has intensified with a separate lawsuit filed by Brian Harpole, escalating tensions.

title: Hindu group bids against Christian-Muslim group for same piece of faith land in UK
description: Northstowe's Hindu community faces a significant challenge as they bid for a faith/community land plot to establish their first temple.  Currently, families travel 40 miles for worship, impacting cultural connection.  Hindu Samaj Northstowe proposes an interfaith hub with prayer rooms and a temple, competing with a church-led bid offering multi-faith spaces.

title: CEO says ‘When Google chief agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
description: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: Maria Sharapova net worth 2026: Grand Slam icon’s empire grows
description: Maria Sharapova, a tennis icon, amassed an estimated $180 million net worth primarily through lucrative endorsement deals and her candy brand, Sugarpova. Her remarkable career included winning all four Grand Slams and reaching world No. 1. After retiring in 2020, she continues to thrive in business and investments, maintaining her global recognition.
